articles.title: OpenAI just released its answer to Claude Mythos
articles.summary: OpenAI launched Daybreak, an AI security initiative using the Codex Security agent to detect and patch vulnerabilities before attackers exploit them by analyzing code and automating threat detection.

articles.title: Here’s what Mira Murati’s AI company is up to
articles.summary: Mira Murati's AI company Thinking Machines announced 'interaction models' that process audio, video, and text in real-time for natural human-AI collaboration.

articles.title: Google stopped a zero-day hack that it says was developed with AI
articles.summary: Google detected and stopped a zero-day exploit created with AI assistance by cybercriminals targeting an open-source administration tool to bypass two-factor authentication in mass attacks.

articles.title: Dessn raises $6M for its production focused design tool
articles.summary: Dessn, an AI-powered design tool startup, raised $6M in funding. The platform integrates directly with production codebases to streamline the design-to-development process.

articles.title: AI voice startup Vapi hits $500M valuation after winning Amazon Ring over 40 rivals
articles.summary: AI voice startup Vapi reached $500M valuation after Amazon Ring selected it over 40 competitors. The company's enterprise business grew 10-fold since early 2025 as companies adopt AI agents for customer support and sales.

articles.title: Thinking Machines wants to build an AI that actually listens while it talks
articles.summary: Thinking Machines is developing an AI model that processes input and generates responses simultaneously, mimicking natural conversation like phone calls rather than sequential text exchanges.

articles.title: Railway secures $100 million to challenge AWS with AI-native cloud infrastructure
articles.summary: Railway, a cloud platform with 2 million developers, raised $100 million Series B to build AI-native infrastructure challenging AWS and Google Cloud by addressing legacy platform limitations.

articles.title: Claude Code costs up to $200 a month. Goose does the same thing for free.
articles.summary: Claude Code charges $20-$200 monthly for AI coding features, while Goose offers identical functionality as a free open-source alternative that runs locally without subscription fees.

articles.title: Listen Labs raises $69M after viral billboard hiring stunt to scale AI customer interviews
articles.summary: Listen Labs raised $69M Series B funding after a viral billboard hiring campaign featuring AI tokens and a coding challenge. The unconventional recruitment stunt in San Francisco attracted thousands of engineers and helped the startup scale.

articles.title: Linux bitten by second severe vulnerability in as many weeks
articles.summary: Linux faces a second severe vulnerability in weeks called Dirty Frag, allowing low-privilege users and containers to gain root access. Exploit code leaked online, and Microsoft reports active exploitation attempts across Linux distributions.

articles.title: Chaos erupts as cyberattack disrupts learning platform Canvas amid finals
articles.summary: A cyberattack disrupted Canvas learning platform during US finals, forcing schools offline. Instructure restored service Friday after identifying unauthorized network activity and data exposure including usernames and emails.

articles.title: Mozilla says 271 vulnerabilities found by Mythos have "almost no false positives"
articles.summary: Mozilla's AI vulnerability detection tool Mythos found 271 Firefox security flaws with minimal false positives over two months, demonstrating significant progress in AI-assisted security testing despite initial skepticism.

articles.title: The Download: a Nobel winner on AI, and the case for fixing everything
articles.summary: Nobel-winning economist Daron Acemoglu argues AI will provide limited productivity gains and won't eliminate human work, a view supported by recent data despite industry optimism.

articles.title: Three things in AI to watch, according to a Nobel-winning economist
articles.summary: Nobel Prize-winning economist Daron Acemoglu challenges Silicon Valley's AI hype, arguing AI will only modestly boost US productivity and won't eliminate human work, contrary to tech CEO predictions.

articles.title: Fostering breakthrough AI innovation through customer-back engineering
articles.summary: Organizations waste digital investments by starting with technology instead of customer needs. Successful AI implementation requires 'customer-back engineering' that prioritizes customer problems first.

articles.title: Building Blocks for Foundation Model Training and Inference on AWS
articles.summary: AWS introduces building blocks for training and deploying foundation models, enabling developers to more efficiently build and run large-scale AI systems on their cloud infrastructure.

articles.title: EMO: Pretraining mixture of experts for emergent modularity
articles.summary: EMO introduces a pretraining approach for mixture of experts models that promotes emergent modularity. This technique enables specialized expert networks that improve efficiency and performance in large-scale AI systems.

articles.title: vLLM V0 to V1: Correctness Before Corrections in RL
articles.summary: vLLM advances from V0 to V1 by prioritizing correctness in reinforcement learning before applying corrections. The update emphasizes accuracy improvements and proper validation protocols.

articles.title: A conversation with Kevin Scott: What’s next in AI
articles.summary: Kevin Scott discusses the future direction of AI technology and its developments. The conversation explores upcoming trends and innovations in the artificial intelligence field.

articles.title: From Hot Wheels to handling content: How brands are using Microsoft AI to be more productive and imaginative
articles.summary: Brands like Hot Wheels leverage Microsoft AI tools to enhance productivity and creativity in content creation and handling. Microsoft AI enables businesses to streamline workflows and boost imaginative output.

articles.title: Microsoft open sources its ‘farm of the future’ toolkit
articles.summary: Microsoft released open-source toolkit for agricultural innovation, promoting sustainable farming practices through AI technology and digital tools.

articles.title: The new AI-powered Google Finance is expanding to Europe.
articles.summary: AI-powered Google Finance is expanding to Europe this week with full local language support and enhanced capabilities. The reimagined platform offers powerful financial tools to European users.

articles.title: See what happens when creative legends use AI to make ads for small businesses.
articles.summary: Google launches The Small Brief, partnering creative advertising legends to use AI for crafting ads for small businesses they support, showcasing innovative approaches to local business promotion.

articles.title: 5 gardening tips you can try right in Search
articles.summary: Google's AI Mode, Search Live, and Shopping features offer gardening tips to help plants thrive. The article highlights top ways to use these tools for gardening success.

articles.title: AlphaEvolve: How our Gemini-powered coding agent is scaling impact across fields
articles.summary: AlphaEvolve, powered by Gemini, is a coding agent scaling impact across business, infrastructure, and science through advanced algorithms.

articles.title: Enabling a new model for healthcare with AI co-clinician
articles.summary: Researchers are developing an AI co-clinician to augment healthcare delivery. The initiative explores how artificial intelligence can support and enhance clinical decision-making.

articles.title: Announcing our partnership with the Republic of Korea
articles.summary: Google DeepMind partners with the Republic of Korea to accelerate scientific breakthroughs using advanced AI models.

articles.title: Last Week in AI #340 - OpenAI vs Musk + Microsoft, DeepSeek v4, Vision Banana
articles.summary: Elon Musk testified in his lawsuit against OpenAI and Sam Altman, claiming the company misappropriated charity resources. The trial revealed evidence including settlement discussions and allegations that xAI used OpenAI models for training.

articles.title: LWiAI Podcast #243 - GPT 5.5, DeepSeek V4, AI safety sabotage
articles.summary: OpenAI released GPT-5.5 with coding improvements and safety monitoring features at higher pricing. xAI launched Grok Voice Think Fast 1.0 for real-time voice agents with major benchmark improvements.

articles.title: LWiAI Podcast #242 - ChatGPT Images 2.0, Qwen 3.6 Max, Kimi-K2.6
articles.summary: LWiAI Podcast #242 discusses major AI developments including OpenAI's new ChatGPT image model with improved text accuracy, Qwen 3.6 Max, and Kimi-K2.6. The episode covers recent breakthroughs in image generation and Chinese AI models.

articles.title: Hugging Face hosted malicious software masquerading as OpenAI release
articles.summary: A malicious Hugging Face repository impersonating OpenAI's Privacy Filter delivered infostealer malware to Windows machines, accumulating approximately 244,000 downloads before removal.

articles.title: Laserfiche unveils AI agents for natural language workflows
articles.summary: Laserfiche released AI agents that execute tasks through natural language prompts while maintaining security and compliance. These intelligent assistants automate workflows and reduce manual work by leveraging generative LLM reasoning models.

articles.title: AI automates HR compliance, except for the area tech companies need
articles.summary: AI automates most HR compliance tasks, but UK tech companies struggle with manual sponsor licence management for international talent, creating a paradox where automation leaders cannot automate their own immigration compliance.

articles.title: Which Agent Causes Task Failures and When?Researchers from PSU and Duke explores automated failure attribution of LLM Multi-Agent Systems
articles.summary: Researchers from PSU and Duke developed methods to automatically identify which agent causes task failures in LLM multi-agent systems and when failures occur, addressing debugging challenges in collaborative AI systems.

articles.title: ByteDance Introduces Astra: A Dual-Model Architecture for Autonomous Robot Navigation
articles.summary: ByteDance introduced Astra, a dual-model architecture for autonomous robot navigation that addresses key challenges in complex indoor environments by answering localization and pathfinding questions.

articles.title: MIT Researchers Unveil “SEAL”: A New Step Towards Self-Improving AI
articles.summary: MIT researchers introduced SEAL, a framework enabling large language models to self-improve by generating training data through self-editing and updating their own weights via reinforcement learning.

articles.title: Google brings multi-token prediction Gemma 4 LLMs
articles.summary: Google's Gemma 4 introduces multi-token prediction and DFlash technology, achieving 3-6x faster throughput for local language models through community-driven optimization.

articles.title: How Memory Sparse Attention scales LLM memory to 100 million tokens
articles.summary: Memory Sparse Attention enables LLMs to process 100 million tokens while maintaining accuracy, dramatically expanding context window capabilities.

articles.title: Claude Code is leaking API keys into public package registries
articles.summary: A study reveals that Claude Code inadvertently leaks sensitive API keys to public code repositories and package registries, posing security risks.

articles.title: Tilde Research Introduces Aurora: A Leverage-Aware Optimizer That Fixes a Hidden Neuron Death Problem in Muon
articles.summary: Tilde Research introduced Aurora, a new optimizer that fixes a neuron death problem in the popular Muon optimizer. Aurora achieves state-of-the-art results on nanoGPT benchmarks with improved neural network training.

articles.title: A Coding Implementation to Portfolio Optimization with skfolio for Building Testing, Tuning, and Comparing Modern Investment Strategies
articles.summary: This tutorial explores skfolio, a scikit-learn compatible library for portfolio optimization. It covers building investment strategies, from baseline portfolios to advanced techniques like mean-variance optimization, risk-parity, hierarchical clustering, and walk-forward validation.

articles.title: OpenAI Introduces Daybreak: A Cybersecurity Initiative That Puts Codex Security at the Center of Vulnerability Detection and Patch Validation
articles.summary: OpenAI launched Daybreak, a cybersecurity initiative combining frontier AI models with Codex Security to detect and patch software vulnerabilities early in development. The program shifts security from reactive to proactive, integrating vulnerability management into the development cycle.

articles.title: I've tested dozens of power stations - here's how I'm preparing for summer blackouts
articles.summary: Summer blackouts are imminent. Prepare backup power solutions like portable power stations now rather than waiting for outages to occur.

articles.title: Why business architects are poised to lead the corporate AI revolution
articles.summary: Business architects are positioned to lead corporate AI adoption by leveraging deep domain knowledge and elevating human skills. Their expertise enables organizations to effectively implement AI solutions aligned with business objectives.

articles.title: Linux Mint vs. Elementary OS: I compared both distros, and here's my advice
articles.summary: Linux Mint and Elementary OS are both user-friendly Linux distributions. The best choice depends on your specific needs and starting point as a user.

articles.title: LLM Observability Tools for Reliable AI Applications
articles.summary: LLMs now power diverse applications including customer service bots and autonomous coding agents, requiring observability tools to ensure reliability.

articles.title: Implementing Prompt Compression to Reduce Agentic Loop Costs
articles.summary: Prompt compression techniques reduce costs of agentic loops in production by minimizing token usage for LLM calls and API interactions where billing depends on token consumption.

articles.title: Implementing Permission-Gated Tool Calling in Python Agents
articles.summary: This article discusses implementing permission-gated tool calling in Python agents, enabling AI agents to move beyond passive chatbots by controlling access to specific tools and actions.

articles.title: Proxy-Pointer Framework for Structure-Aware Enterprise Document Intelligence
articles.summary: A new Proxy-Pointer Framework enables structure-aware analysis of enterprise documents like contracts and research papers through hierarchical understanding and comparison capabilities.

articles.title: Your First WebAssembly Program and Web App (Written, Tested, and Deployed Entirely in the Web Browser)
articles.summary: Learn to compile and run C code as WebAssembly using Emscripten and GitHub Codespaces entirely in your browser without local installation.

articles.title: Learning Word Vectors for Sentiment Analysis: A Python Reproduction
articles.summary: Tutorial on building sentiment-aware word vectors from IMDb reviews using semantic learning and SVM classification in Python.

articles.title: NVIDIA and SAP Bring Trust to Specialized Agents
articles.summary: NVIDIA and SAP expand collaboration to deploy specialized AI agents in enterprise systems with security controls. SAP integrates NVIDIA's OpenShell runtime into its Business AI Platform for secure autonomous agent development.

articles.title: ‘Your Career Starts at the Beginning of the AI Revolution,’ NVIDIA CEO Tells Graduates
articles.summary: NVIDIA CEO Jensen Huang told Carnegie Mellon graduates they're entering an extraordinary moment at the beginning of the AI revolution with unprecedented tools and opportunities to shape the future.

articles.title: Powering the Next American Century: US Energy Secretary Chris Wright and NVIDIA’s Ian Buck on the Genesis Mission
articles.summary: US Energy Secretary Chris Wright and NVIDIA's Ian Buck argue that American AI leadership depends on energy leadership, with AI helping to generate the energy it requires.

articles.title: Building web search-enabled agents with Strands and Exa
articles.summary: Exa integration with Strands Agents SDK provides AI-native web search designed for agent workflows, delivering structured content optimized for LLMs without requiring custom parsing or crawling layers.

articles.title: Introducing Claude Platform on AWS: Anthropic’s native platform, through your AWS account
articles.summary: Anthropic launches Claude Platform on AWS, enabling customers to access Claude's native platform directly through their AWS accounts without separate credentials or billing. AWS becomes the first cloud provider offering this integrated native Claude experience.

articles.title: Manufacturing intelligence with Amazon Nova Multimodal Embeddings
articles.summary: Amazon Nova Multimodal Embeddings enables manufacturing companies to search technical documents combining text and images through a unified vector space, allowing text queries to retrieve diagrams and vice versa.

articles.title: Introducing NVIDIA Fleet Intelligence for Real-Time GPU Fleet Visibility and Optimization
articles.summary: NVIDIA Fleet Intelligence enables real-time visibility and optimization of large GPU fleets, helping teams manage heterogeneous hardware, software stacks, and power constraints at scale.

articles.title: Improving Bash Generation in Small Language Models with Grammar-Constrained Decoding
articles.summary: Researchers improve Bash command generation in small language models using grammar-constrained decoding, enabling AI agents to produce valid executable shell commands reliably.

articles.title: Streaming Tokens and Tools: Multi-Turn Agentic Harness Support in NVIDIA Dynamo
articles.summary: NVIDIA Dynamo introduces streaming tokens and tools for multi-turn agentic interactions, enabling assistants to interleave reasoning with tool calls while preserving structured conversation flow.

articles.title: How generational differences affect consumer attitudes towards ads
articles.summary: A research study examines how generational differences influence consumer attitudes toward ads across various social media platforms, partnering with CrowdDNA.

articles.title: Every tree counts
articles.summary: Meta aims for net zero emissions by 2030 by developing carbon footprint mitigation technology and sharing it openly.

articles.title: How a non-traditional background led to cutting-edge XR tech
articles.summary: An individual with unconventional background developed innovative extended reality technology, demonstrating how diverse experiences can drive technological advancement in the XR field.

articles.title: Adaptive Parallel Reasoning: The Next Paradigm in Efficient Inference Scaling
articles.summary: Adaptive Parallel Reasoning enables AI models to autonomously decide when to decompose tasks into parallel subtasks and manage concurrent processing threads based on problem requirements, advancing efficient inference scaling.

articles.title: Gradient-based Planning for World Models at Longer Horizons
articles.summary: GRASP is a new gradient-based planner for world models enabling practical long-horizon planning through parallel trajectory optimization, stochastic exploration, and improved gradient shaping for clean action signals.

articles.title: Identifying Interactions at Scale for LLMs
articles.summary: Researchers developed methods to identify interactions at scale in Large Language Models to improve interpretability and understand how these complex systems make decisions, advancing safer and more trustworthy AI.
